actor_claims.quote_text: The Board, together with Company management and with the assistance of the Company’s advisors, periodically reviews and considers various strategic and other opportunities available to the Company to enhance stockholder value
The advisory committee authorized Company management to inquire whether Barclays would be available to act as a financial advisor to the Company in connection with a potential strategic transaction.
representatives of the Company’s outside legal counsel, Goodwin Procter LLP (“ Goodwin ”), were in attendance.
representatives of Thoma Bravo informally approached and had brief meetings with representatives of the Company
Sponsor A indicated a price of $16.50 per share.
Sponsor B indicated a range of $17.00 - $18.00 per share.

actor_claims.actor_label: private equity funds affiliated with Thoma Bravo
actor_claims.quote_text: private equity funds affiliated with Thoma Bravo acquired an aggregate of 1,213,066 shares of Imprivata common stock

actor_relation_claims.quote_text: recommended the engagement of Barclays as the Company’s financial advisor to assist the Board in its evaluation of Thoma Bravo’s indication of interest and any other strategic alternatives that might be available to the Company.
representatives of the Company’s outside legal counsel, Goodwin Procter LLP (“ Goodwin ”), were in attendance.
private equity funds affiliated with Thoma Bravo acquired an aggregate of 1,213,066 shares of Imprivata common stock
actor_relation_claims.subject_label: private equity funds affiliated with Thoma Bravo

actor_relation_claims.quote_text: the Company permitted two sources of potential debt financing, each of which Barclays identified as parties that could potentially provide debt financing to Thoma Bravo, to join as parties under the confidentiality agreement between the Company and Thoma Bravo.
actor_relation_claims.subject_label: two sources of potential debt financing

bid_claims.quote_text: On March 9, 2016, Thoma Bravo sent an unsolicited, non-binding indication of interest letter addressed to the Board indicating that Thoma Bravo would be interested in acquiring the Company for cash at a purchase price of $15.00 per share of Imprivata’s common stock.
Sponsor A indicated a price of $16.50 per share.
Thoma Bravo indicated a price of $17.25 per share, and also provided a form of equity commitment letter and draft merger agreement.
Sponsor B indicated a range of $17.00 - $18.00 per share.

Thoma Bravo’s bid was at a price of $19.00 per share in cash, provided for an equity commitment for the entire purchase price and indicated that Thoma Bravo had completed all of its due diligence and was prepared to execute its revised draft of the merger agreement and announce the transaction prior to the market open on July 11, 2016.

Later on July 9, 2016, Barclays received a revised written, non-binding proposal from Thoma Bravo setting forth its best and final offer of $19.25 per share, together with a revised draft of the equity commitment letter for the entire purchase price for the Company.

event_claims.description: Thoma Bravo sent an unsolicited non-binding indication of interest to acquire the Company for cash at $15.00 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: On March 9, 2016, Thoma Bravo sent an unsolicited, non-binding indication of interest letter addressed to the Board indicating that Thoma Bravo would be interested in acquiring the Company for cash at a purchase price of $15.00 per share of Imprivata’s common stock.

event_claims.description: The Board approved contacting parties to gauge interest in a potential transaction, commencing the strategic process.
event_claims.quote_text: The Board also approved the indicative list of parties and determined that Barclays should contact each of the parties on the indicative list on a confidential basis to gauge its interest in a potential transaction with the Company, which we refer to as the “strategic process.”

event_claims.description: Barclays contacted and discussed a potential transaction with 15 potentially interested parties at the Board's direction.
event_claims.quote_text: During the period from May 6 through June 9, 2016, at the direction of the Board, Barclays contacted and had discussions with the 15 potentially interested parties discussed by the Board at its April 28 and May 5, 2016 meetings, including Thoma Bravo.

event_claims.description: Thoma Bravo executed a confidentiality agreement with the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: Of the parties contacted, three strategic parties and four financial sponsors executed confidentiality agreements with the Company, including Thoma Bravo on May 10, 2016.

event_claims.description: Barclays distributed a bid instruction letter to six parties that had executed confidentiality agreements and attended management meetings.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 3, 2016, Barclays distributed a bid instruction letter on behalf of the Company to the six parties that had executed a confidentiality agreement and attended meetings with Company management by that date.

event_claims.actor_label: Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo
event_claims.description: Three financial sponsor parties submitted written preliminary non-binding indications of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 9, 2016, three parties (Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo) presented written preliminary non-binding indications of interest that were each subject to satisfactory completion of due diligence, among other conditions.

event_claims.actor_label: Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo
event_claims.description: The Board authorized Barclays to advance Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo to the second phase of the strategic process.
event_claims.quote_text: Following these discussions, because Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo had submitted proposals within close range of each other, the Board authorized Barclays to advance all three parties to the second phase of the strategic process and to encourage them to indicate a willingness to increase their respective offer prices.

event_claims.description: Sponsor A effectively ceased participating after indicating a second-round bid would not be meaningfully higher.
event_claims.quote_text: Following this discussion there were no further discussions between Sponsor A and Barclays or any other Company representatives.

event_claims.description: Final bid process letters were sent to Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo with a July 8 final bid deadline.
event_claims.quote_text: Later on June 24, 2016, Barclays sent final bid process letters to Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo, requesting marked drafts of the Company’s proposed form of merger agreement (which would be provided in due course) by July 7, 2016, and setting a final bid deadline of July 8, 2016.

event_claims.description: Sponsor B indicated any final bid would be significantly below its prior indication and had no further discussions with Company representatives.
event_claims.quote_text: Following this discussion there were no further discussions between Sponsor B and Barclays or any other Company representatives.

event_claims.description: Only Thoma Bravo submitted a final bid by the final bid date.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 8, 2016, the date by which Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo had been invited to submit their final bids, only Thoma Bravo submitted a bid for the Company.

event_claims.description: Thoma Bravo submitted a revised best and final offer of $19.25 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: Later on July 9, 2016, Barclays received a revised written, non-binding proposal from Thoma Bravo setting forth its best and final offer of $19.25 per share, together with a revised draft of the equity commitment letter for the entire purchase price for the Company.

event_claims.actor_label: two sources of potential debt financing
event_claims.description: Two potential debt financing sources were permitted to join the confidentiality agreement between the Company and Thoma Bravo.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 5 and 6, 2016, in response to Thoma Bravo’s request to work with potential debt financing sources, the Company permitted two sources of potential debt financing, each of which Barclays identified as parties that could potentially provide debt financing to Thoma Bravo, to join as parties under the confidentiality agreement between the Company and Thoma Bravo.

participation_count_claims.quote_text: During the period from May 6 through June 9, 2016, at the direction of the Board, Barclays contacted and had discussions with the 15 potentially interested parties discussed by the Board at its April 28 and May 5, 2016 meetings, including Thoma Bravo.
Of the parties contacted, three strategic parties and four financial sponsors executed confidentiality agreements with the Company, including Thoma Bravo on May 10, 2016.
Of the parties contacted, three strategic parties and four financial sponsors executed confidentiality agreements with the Company, including Thoma Bravo on May 10, 2016.

On June 3, 2016, Barclays distributed a bid instruction letter on behalf of the Company to the six parties that had executed a confidentiality agreement and attended meetings with Company management by that date.

On June 9, 2016, three parties (Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo) presented written preliminary non-binding indications of interest that were each subject to satisfactory completion of due diligence, among other conditions.

Following these discussions, because Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo had submitted proposals within close range of each other, the Board authorized Barclays to advance all three parties to the second phase of the strategic process and to encourage them to indicate a willingness to increase their respective offer prices.

Later on June 24, 2016, Barclays sent final bid process letters to Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo, requesting marked drafts of the Company’s proposed form of merger agreement (which would be provided in due course) by July 7, 2016, and setting a final bid deadline of July 8, 2016.
On July 8, 2016, the date by which Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo had been invited to submit their final bids, only Thoma Bravo submitted a bid for the Company.
